name: The Castle of Xàtiva — Circular from Benigànim
name_original: El Castell de Xàtiva — Circular desde Benigànim
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Benigànim are situated in the Valle de Albaida, a region characterized by varied terrain suitable for different cycling preferences. The area features modest climbs and is close to medium-sized mountains within the Sierra Calderona and Sierra d'Espada ranges, offering pine forests and panoramic viewpoints. Natural features like La Solana, Sierra Gorda, and Embalse de Bellús contribute to diverse cycling landscapes. Best touring cycling routes around Benigànim The most popular touring cycling route is The Castle of Xàtiva — Circular from Benigànim, a 19.4 miles (31.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete, leading to the historic Xàtiva castle. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Serra de Quatretonda – Castell del Xiu loop from Benigànim, a difficult 24.9 miles (40.2 km) path offering scenic views from the mountain ridge. Local touring cyclists also love the Cova Negra – Fountain of the 25 Spouts loop from Benigànim, a 23.8 miles (38.3 km) trail leading through natural features and local water sources, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Benigànim is defined by varied terrain, pine forests, and mountain ridges, with routes offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Benigànim are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 8 reviews. More than 250 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Benigànim's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Benigànim?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Benigànim, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these routes over 250 times, providing valuable insights and ratings.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Benigànim?
A: Benigànim benefits from a Mediterranean climate, making it suitable for touring cycling throughout the year. The weather is generally pleasant, though spring and autumn offer particularly comfortable temperatures for longer rides.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Benigànim?
A: Yes, while many routes in the area are challenging, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. These routes often feature mostly paved surfaces, making them accessible for those new to touring cycling or families looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on touring cycling routes near Benigànim?
A: The region offers diverse natural landscapes. You can cycle through areas like La Solana, explore the Sierra Gorda mountain ridge, or visit the scenic &lt;strong&gt;Embalse de Bellús&lt;/strong&gt; (Bellús Lake). Routes may also pass by highlights such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5292827" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellús Reservoir and spillway&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4836497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sempere Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4042646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bellús Reservoir Pine Forest&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to visit while cycling around Benigànim?
A: Absolutely. Benigànim and its surroundings are rich in history. You can explore the historic district of La Illeta, visit churches like the San Miguel Arcángel Parish Church, or see the former Franciscan Convent. A notable route, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e917555266/the-castle-of-xativa-circular-from-beniganim" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Castle of Xàtiva — Circular from Benigànim&lt;/a&gt;, leads to the historic Xàtiva castle, offering stunning views and a glimpse into the past. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7459120" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Ollería&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find good viewpoints along the touring cycling routes in Benigànim?
A: Many routes offer rewarding viewpoints. The broader Valencia province, including areas like Sierra Calderona and Sierra d'Espada, features panoramic vistas. Closer to Benigànim, the Stations of the Cross offer spectacular views over the town. Specific highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4836497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sempere Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from Alt de l’Aldaia&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5688912" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Miradors&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Benigànim?
A: Yes, several touring cycling routes around Benigànim are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14663737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serra de Quatretonda – Castell del Xiu loop from Benigànim&lt;/a&gt;, which offers scenic views from the mountain ridge.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Benigànim?
A: The routes around Benigànim cater to various fitness levels. While there is one easy route, the majority are more challenging, with 7 moderate and 30 difficult routes. These often involve significant elevation gains, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14666793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Corpus Christi Convent – Hermitage of Saint Joseph loop from Benigànim&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 1500 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about touring cycling in Benigànim?
A: The komoot community rates the touring cycling routes around Benigànim highly, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the blend of natural beauty with cultural landmarks, and the opportunities for challenging rides through pine forests and along mountain ridges.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by reservoirs or lakes?
A: Yes, you can find routes that incorporate the region's beautiful reservoirs and lakes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38258604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sempere Reservoir Viewpoint – Bellús Reservoir Pine Forest loop from Benigànim&lt;/a&gt; offers views of these water bodies and surrounding natural areas.

Q: Can I find routes that explore local water sources or natural springs?
A: Yes, the region is known for its natural water sources. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21943166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Negra – Fountain of the 25 Spouts loop from Benigànim&lt;/a&gt; is a great option, leading through natural features and highlighting local water sources, including the famous Fountain of the 25 Spouts.
tips: The "Castell de Xàtiva" is located on the old road Via Augusta, which leads from Rome over the Pyrenees and the Mediterranean coast down to Cartagena and Cádiz. It thus held a very favorable strategic position and for a long time was considered one of the strongest fortresses in the Kingdom of Valencia. Even today it is still a grandiose and imposing place that is definitely worth seeing.
In the vicinity of Xàtiva is the Cova Negra, declared a Municipal Natural Area in 2006, an essential visit.
